title: 1001 comics you must read before you die
display_title: 1001 comics you must read before you die
wikitext:
[]: '1001 comics you must read before you die'             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Dec 18, 2011 6:38 pm http://www.amazon.co.uk/1001-Comic-Books-Must-Before/dp/1844036987/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1324251621&sr=8-1 Page 648: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Dec 18, 2011 6:41 pm Right next to the Boondocks........great.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Dec 18, 2011 6:45 pm Should point out that the entries are arranged by date of release, not on any sort of merit. Oh and if anyone interested in knowing if a particular title has an entry, give me a shout :)

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Sun Dec 18, 2011 6:48 pm Jayfive wrote: Should point out that the entries are arranged by date of release, not on any sort of merit. Oh and if anyone interested in knowing if a particular title has an entry, give me a shout :)

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Dec 18, 2011 6:52 pm ^ I'm interested as well, wonder what it is?

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Dec 18, 2011 7:01 pm The first entry, which is in the 'Pre-1930' section is: 'The Adventures of Mr Obadiah Oldbuck' (1837)

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Sun Dec 18, 2011 7:04 pm ^ That makes sense. If that's the earliest entry, what's the latest entry then?

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Dec 18, 2011 7:19 pm This: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Habibi_%28graphic_novel%29

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Dec 18, 2011 7:28 pm I've never Heard of it. but I might check it out now.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Sun Dec 18, 2011 7:35 pm Also, (sorry for asking so much, but maybe I should get this book), is Bone by Jeff Smith on this? Also, Watchmen and the Killing Joke have to be in there, right?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Dec 18, 2011 9:34 pm Is there any other manga listed in the book?

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Dec 19, 2011 4:04 am Quite a lot of manga in there - barefoot gen, ghost in the shell, akira, death note, dragonball, sailor moon - all the ones you might expect plus a fair few more 'vintage' or obscure titles. Ill try and do a full list of Japanese titles in there if I get chance. Also, Watchmen and the Killing Joke have to be in there, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Mon Dec 19, 2011 7:09 am I Hope you don't mind me asking but, wouldn't you know if The Sandman is in there too? And also, are there any argentinian comics in there too? (i'm thinking more specifically Corto Maltese and Perramus)

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Dec 19, 2011 9:52 am Dream wrote: I Hope you don't mind me asking but, wouldn't you know if The Sandman is in there too?

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Mon Dec 19, 2011 10:40 am Y'know, I don't own this yet. I've got 1001 Albums You Must Hear Before You Die , however. Dream wrote: I Hope you don't mind me asking but, wouldn't you know if The Sandman is in there too?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Dec 19, 2011 10:51 am AngelNo13Bardiel wrote: Y'know, I don't own this yet. I've got 1001 Albums You Must Hear Before You Die , however.

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Mon Dec 19, 2011 2:46 pm No Elfen Lied and Dr.Mcninja. Meh.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Dec 19, 2011 5:10 pm ^No surprise.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Mon Dec 19, 2011 6:20 pm Yay! Corto Maltese actually is in there! and a lot of others i didn't even though they would be. Hell, even the Eternaut is here!

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Tue Dec 20, 2011 8:28 am Incidently the last time I saw Eva mentioned in print was a Japan pocket tourist guide which included it in a list 'films featured in tokyo' Sometimes I think being a western eva fan is what a Japanese star wars fan would be like coming to the west. 'Look! Look! Its mentioned in stuff! Theres books about it and everything!'
